acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReplRig no longer relies on check-then-bind port selection for daemon or proxy listeners
- [ ] Tailnet and repl integration tests stay isolated under parallel nextest runs
- [ ] Canonical fast and slow tiers stay green after the port-allocation change
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs` allocates daemon and proxy listen ports with `pick_port()`: bind `127.0.0.1:0`, read the port, drop the listener, then bind that address for real later. Under parallel nextest runs another test can claim the same port in the gap, turning replication and proxy setup into a race.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/bin/tailnet_proxy.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/repl/server.rs`
design: Stop probing free ports up front. Teach the harness to use actual bound sockets or `127.0.0.1:0` end-to-end, then publish the real listen address back into fixture metadata/config before peers reload replication. The fix should remove the check-then-bind gap for both daemon listeners and tailnet proxies.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Closed from /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs.

Landed root-cause fix:
- `ReplRig` no longer uses `pick_port()` for daemon or proxy listeners. Nodes now boot on `127.0.0.1:0`, publish their bound replication address through admin status, and only then write peer config.
- `tailnet_proxy` now writes its actual bound listen address into the ready file, and the fixture consumes that instead of trusting a guessed port.
- Initial peer publication uses a peers-only reload path so the harness can update outbound peers without tearing down a healthy inbound listener.
- Tailnet fault-injection tests are fenced into the `tailnet-fault-injection` nextest group so the fast tier stops oversubscribing the proxy-heavy cluster.

Verification:
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e::repl_daemon_stress_wal_rotation_roundtrip -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e::repl_checkpoint_bootstrap_under_churn -- --nocapture`
- `env BD_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-config BD_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-runtime XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/codex-ambient-xdg GIT_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-dir GIT_WORK_TREE=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-worktree cargo xtest --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `42.099s`
- `env BD_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-config BD_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-runtime XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/codex-ambient-xdg GIT_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-dir GIT_WORK_TREE=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-worktree cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `41.689s`

title: Eliminate ReplRig check-then-bind port races in replication harness
